_note: 现任主帅;换帅经 --set 更新(旧任压进 history,只增不改)。下游:match_header 主帅行。
brief: Moroccan coach Jamal Sellami (career began 2011) took over Jordan in June 2024, securing their first-ever 2026 World Cup qualification. The former Morocco international, a 1998 World Cup player, succeeded Hussein Ammouta. Sellami aims to replicate Morocco's 2022 heroics and believes Jordan can deliver shock upsets comparable to Algeria 1982, Cameroon 1990, or Senegal 2002. He is without star forward Yazan Al-Naimat (ACL injury, December). Primary attacking threats are Ali Olwan (9 qualifying goals, recovered from Achilles injury) and Mousa Al-Tamari. The 26-man squad includes Raja Casablanca's Mohammed Abu Zureiq ("Sharara"). Defender Mohammad Taha, 20, was promoted after forward Ibrahim Sabra withdrew with an ankle ligament rupture.

coach_xverify.discrepancy: 所有来源一致声称Jamal Sellami是约旦队主教练，但所提及的'2026年世界杯'背景与事实不符（约旦尚未获得参赛资格），信息可能为假设性报道或存在事实错误
